title: 2025-2026 Respiratory Disease Season Outlook - December Update | CFA: Qualitative Assessments | CDC

National COVID-19 hospitalizations, as reported by NHSN, are displayed with the blue line (peak of 3.25 per 100,000). The model was run on August 18, 2025, using data through August 9, 2025. Historical COVID-19 hospitalization data (NHSN and NSSP-inferred) are current as of December 2, 2025. Show More 
Influenza outlook 
CDC expects that the severity of the 2025-2026 influenza season will most likely be classified as moderate across all ages, with low confidence , based on historical trends, expert opinion, and recent surveillance data trends. Our confidence in this assessment is low, downgraded from moderate confidence in our initial influenza outlook, due to uncertainties in the potential impact of circulating influenza viruses on U.S. population immunity and vaccine effectiveness. As of December 16, epidemic trend estimates indicate that influenza infections are growing or likely growing in 47 states and the timing of this increase in activity is similar to several past seasons.
